g.text: very strong — device likely within tens to ~200 m
uses ~0.001% of the 1% EU868 duty-cycle budget
32-bit address — assigned at join (OTAA) or provisioned (ABP)
upper 7 bits of DevAddr — identifies operator network
Regular uplink: device is sending sensor/application data to the network.
Payload is AES-128 encrypted — content is not recoverable without the AppSKey.
ADR ON: network server is actively managing SF and TX power for this device.
MAC commands in uplink → device is responding to network configuration requests.
Very strong link margin → device is probably close (< 200 m or same building).
